sourceMovie.title: Tootsie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Tootsie (1982) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Friends with Benefits
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tootsie clicked for you, Friends with Benefits (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Tootsie?
faq.answer: Try Friends with Benefits, The Lobster, Enchanted—each shares DNA with Tootsie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Tootsie?
faq.answer: Tootsie (1982) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Tootsie worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
